title: carex spicata subsp. spicata
intro: Carex spicata subsp. spicata, also known as the Spiked Sedge, is a type of plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Carex spicata subsp. spicata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural diuretic. Carex spicata subsp. spicata can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's rhizomes and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
